title: gladiolus praecostatus
intro: Gladiolus praecostatus, also known as the spotted corn lily, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Iridaceae family. The plant's rhizomes and roots have been traditionally used in medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Gladiolus praecostatus include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and roots can be dried and used as a tea or tincture, or they can be made into a poultice for topical application.
